perturbation.original_text: 1.20 "Net Revenue" shall have the meaning set forth in Section 5.5 [Net Revenue Defined].
perturbation.changed_text: 1.20 "Net Revenue" shall have the meaning as determined by the Billing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines 'Net Revenue' by referencing Section 5.5. The modified text removes this specific reference, granting the Billing Party sole discretion in determining the meaning of 'Net Revenue'. This introduces ambiguity, as the definition in Section 5.5 no longer applies, and the term's meaning becomes subjective. This contradicts Section 5.4 [Share of Net Revenue], which relies on a clear and objective definition of 'Net Revenue' for revenue sharing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.4 Share of Net Revenue. As financial consideration under this Agreement, Net Revenue will be shared by the parties as follows: (a) For so long as PlanetCAD hosts the Co-Branded Service, during which time PlanetCAD shall be the Billing Party, all Net Revenue derived from sales of the Co-Branded Service to Dassault Systemes Customers shall be apportioned [***] percent ([***]%) to Dassault Systems and [***] percent ([***]%) to PlanetCAD. (b) In the event that Dassault Systemes opts to host the Co-Branded Service pursuant to Section 4.7 [Change of Hosting] herein they shall become the Billing Party and all Net Revenue derived from sales of the Co-Branded Service shall be apportioned [***] percent ([***]%) to Dassault Systems and [***] percent ([***]%) to PlanetCAD.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a fixed definition of 'Net Revenue' tied to Section 5.5 with a definition determined solely by the Billing Party. This directly contradicts Section 5.4, which specifies the apportionment of 'Net Revenue' between the parties. If 'Net Revenue' is subjectively determined by the Billing Party, the apportionment outlined in 5.4 becomes unenforceable, as there's no objective standard for calculating the revenue to be shared. The original text provided a clear definition in 5.5 which makes section 5.4 viable, whereas the changed text removes this reference making Section 5.4 potentially worthless.

perturbation.original_text: 5.7 Records - Audit. The Billing Party shall, for two years following each transaction relating to the Co-Branded Service, keep true and accurate records and books of account of such transaction containing all particulars which may be necessary for the purpose of auditing payments to the Non-Billing Party under this Agreement. During such two-year period, and upon reasonable notice to the Billing Party, the Non-Billing Party shall have the right to have an audit conducted through a licensed independent accounting firm, of any billings, collections, and taxes on such itemized statement, and to examine the records and books of account of the Billing Party in connection therewith.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.7 Records - Audit. The Billing Party shall maintain records related to the Co-Branded Service. Audits are subject to the Billing Party's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text provides a detailed description of the audit process, including the requirement to keep accurate records, the Non-Billing Party's right to audit, and the scope of the audit. The modified text significantly reduces these obligations, stating that 'Audits are subject to the Billing Party's discretion'. This directly contradicts the original intent of enabling the Non-Billing Party to verify the accuracy of payments and creates uncertainty. By giving the Billing Party complete control over audits, it makes it difficult, if not impossible, for the Non-Billing Party to ensure it's receiving its fair share of revenue as outlined in Section 5.4 [Share of Net Revenue].
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.4 Share of Net Revenue. As financial consideration under this Agreement, Net Revenue will be shared by the parties as follows: (a) For so long as PlanetCAD hosts the Co-Branded Service, during which time PlanetCAD shall be the Billing Party, all Net Revenue derived from sales of the Co-Branded Service to Dassault Systemes Customers shall be apportioned [***] percent ([***]%) to Dassault Systems and [***] percent ([***]%) to PlanetCAD. (b) In the event that Dassault Systemes opts to host the Co-Branded Service pursuant to Section 4.7 [Change of Hosting] herein they shall become the Billing Party and all Net Revenue derived from sales of the Co-Branded Service shall be apportioned [***] percent ([***]%) to Dassault Systems and [***] percent ([***]%) to PlanetCAD.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 5.7 grants the Non-Billing Party the right to audit billings and examine records related to the Co-Branded Service. The changed text makes audits subject to the Billing Party's discretion, removing the Non-Billing Party's right and ability to verify the accuracy of revenue calculations. This directly contradicts Section 5.4, which details how Net Revenue will be shared between the parties. Without an independent audit, the Non-Billing Party cannot ensure it is receiving its correct share of revenue, rendering the revenue-sharing agreement in Section 5.4 unenforceable and creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.6 Software Support and Maintenance Obligations of the Parties. For the Term of this Agreement the parties shall cooperate to perform maintenance and support services on their respective software included on and necessary to the operation of the Co-Branded Service. The description of maintenance and support obligations is set forth in the Maintenance and Support Services Schedule, attached hereto as Schedule B and incorporated herein by reference.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.6 Software Support and Maintenance Obligations of the Parties. The parties will address software support and maintenance as needed.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that the parties will cooperate on software maintenance and support, with details in Schedule B. The modified text replaces this with a vague statement, eliminating the requirement for specific cooperation and removing the reference to Schedule B. This contradicts the implied obligation to provide adequate support, potentially impacting the service's quality and reliability. This ambiguity creates a direct contradiction with Section 8.1(b), which warrants that the infrastructure including Third Party Software shall function and perform to the specifications and requirements thereof, as lack of proper maintenance will affect the proper function of the infrastructure and Third Party Software
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 Mutual Representations. Each party represents and warrants to the other party as follows: (a) The performance of this Agreement does not infringe or conflict with any Intellectual Property right of any third party, and (ii) no confidential, proprietary or trade secret information of either party or their personnel that will be used in performing this Agreement has been misappropriated from any third party; and (b) All services, work, obligations or assignments performed by either party under this Agreement will be of professional quality, conforming to generally accepted practices within the industry, and the Infrastructure including Third Party Software shall function and perform to the specifications and requirements thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the software support and maintenance obligations to a vague statement, removing the explicit cooperation requirement outlined in the original text and Schedule B. This directly contradicts Section 8.1(b), which warrants the infrastructure and Third Party Software's performance to specifications, as proper maintenance is crucial for this performance. The absence of a specific maintenance agreement directly contradicts the warranty of functionality.
